extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AVERMAET won from a late-splitting elite group after circuit ramp fractured the field, taking 10 bonus seconds and race lead over SÜTTERLIN.
tactical_patterns: SAGAN deployed two teammates (BENNATI, GAVIRIA) in decisive split, showing Tinkoff prioritized stage over GC defense tactics.
Repeated circuit ramp naturally fragmented favorites without need for attacks; positioning before final lap proved decisive.
VAN AVERMAET sat on wheels until final 200m despite being strongest, extracting maximum bonus seconds risk-free.
SÜTTERLIN lost 7-second GC lead by failing to make circuit split, exposing time trial specialists' vulnerability on punchy circuits.

rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: positive: Delivered when openly stating GC ambitions pre-stage, showing form confidence translates to execution on selective finishes.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Had numerical advantage with two teammates in break but couldn't convert, suggesting sprint hesitation on uphill drags.
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: neutral: Present in decisive move and launched long-range attack inside final kilometer, indicating strong form but poor timing.
rider_signals.SÜTTERLIN Jasha: negative: Lost race lead by missing circuit split despite holding 7 seconds, revealing inability to respond on repeated punchy climbs.
rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: negative: Dropped completely from top-10 GC on circuit stage, exposing fragility despite time trial strength earlier in race.
